title: ptarmica schouwii
intro: Ptarmica schouwii, also known as Indian or Chinese feverfew, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe, Asia, and North America. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, specifically its leaves and flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Ptarmica schouwii include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and alleviating menstrual cramps, as well as its potential in preventing migraine attacks and reducing inflammation. Ptarmica schouwii is typically prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or it can be used as a tincture or added to capsules or supplements.
